chunk_summary: This section focuses on growth monitoring frequency and referral triggers within the ICDS Anganwadi Service Package, emphasizing community-based and facility-based management of acute malnutrition in children under public health and pediatric nutrition frameworks.
learning_objectives: Understand the cadence of growth monitoring in ICDS Anganwadi centers.
Identify clinical referral triggers for children with severe acute malnutrition (SAM).
Learn community-based management protocols for acute malnutrition (CMAM).
Explore facility-based management guidelines for SAM as per NHM operational standards.
key_facts: Growth monitoring is conducted monthly for children under 5 years in Anganwadi centers.
Referral triggers include weight-for-height Z-score < -3 SD or visible signs of severe wasting.
Community-based management of acute malnutrition (CMAM) is outlined in the October 2023 protocol.
Facility-based management of SAM includes therapeutic feeding and medical care per NHM guidelines.
POSHAN ILA Module 08 provides standardized tools for growth assessment and escalation decisions.
reference_quote: Community-based management of acute malnutrition protocol (CMAM) (10 Oct 2023) emphasizes early identification and referral of children with severe acute malnutrition through Anganwadi centers.
document_key_topics: Growth monitoring cadence in ICDS Anganwadi centers
Referral triggers for severe acute malnutrition (SAM)
